Root Cause (first observed failure):

action.hypothesis: `product_codebook_topk` did not fail on quality; it failed because the runtime still does not recognize the new architecture name. The next highest-value move is a narrow compatibility repair in the architecture validation/factory path so the discrete additive-codebook family can produce a real proxy signal before we decide whether to keep or archive this branch.
action.summary: Apply a minimal `code_fix` under `sparsify/` to fully register `product_codebook_topk` in the config and sparse-coder factory paths, then rerun the same K=128 proxy recipe. This preserves experiment comparability, resolves the concrete blocker from round 40, and converts an invalid crash into an informative architecture result.
action.expected_win: Best case: the family runs cleanly and yields a real proxy FVU, potentially showing whether additive product-codebooks can beat recent alternatives like `batch_topk` and `bucketed_topk`. Failure case: the repaired run is clearly uncompetitive, letting us archive the family with confidence instead of losing another round to registration issues.
action.self_review: This round does not resume parameter tweaking and does not introduce a new confounded architecture change. It addresses the explicit blocker from round 40 with the smallest repair needed to restore valid architecture comparison.
notes_to_memory: Round 41 is a repair-only follow-up for `product_codebook_topk` after round 40 crashed with an unknown-architecture ValueError.
The blocker is registration/compatibility, not quality, so this round should be treated as infra repair rather than an architecture verdict.
Do not judge the discrete additive-codebook direction until a repaired proxy run completes successfully.
next_hypotheses: If the repaired `product_codebook_topk` run is competitive at K=128 proxy, promote it to full before any family-local tuning.

If the repaired `product_codebook_topk` run completes but remains clearly behind, archive additive product-codebook routing and pivot to a different fundamentally different direction such as preprocessing or output-aware objectives.

If the repair exposes a second architecture-specific runtime issue, continue with one more narrow `code_fix` only if it is still local to `product_codebook_topk` registration or forward/backward compatibility.
